record_identity.title: Sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane primitive

summary: This patch records the implemented Caelestis corpus repair responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0024 and VIGIL-2026-PROP-0011. The repair adds explicit coverage for sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane governance, treating FedRAMP as the evidenced example rather than the name or outer boundary of the doctrine. It implements a governance failure-taxonomy subtype for Sovereign Assurance Boundary Porosity Failure and a SECURITY boundary primitive for Sovereign Assurance Boundary, while preserving reverse leakage and authority bleed-through as risk vectors rather than confirmed harms unless separately evidenced.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The patch closes the primitive gap identified by FM-0024 and PROP-0011. CAM can now distinguish ordinary outage or feature degradation from assurance-wrapper failure, audit-plane degradation, cross-lane incident opacity, governed porosity gaps, feature parity fragility, and sovereign/non-public runtime lane continuity issues without alleging data leakage, military involvement, unlawful access, or an actual compliance breach absent separate evidence.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis Architecture / Operations failure taxonomy / Security boundary primitives / sovereign assurance boundary porosity / regulated runtime lanes

change_details.change_summary: The Caelestis corpus was updated to add explicit coverage for sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane governance. OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 adds §3.8.15 Sovereign Assurance Boundary Porosity Failure for degraded, opaque, or insufficiently governed porosity between public, enterprise, sovereign, public-sector, defence-adjacent, regulated, compliance-bound, or institution-specific AI runtime lanes. SECURITY-002-PLATINUM adds §2.2.13 Sovereign Assurance Boundary as a boundary primitive for runtime environments operating under legally, institutionally, operationally, or security-conditioned obligations distinct from the public/commercial baseline.
change_details.implementation_notes: FedRAMP remains the evidenced example and not the doctrine name or outer boundary. The patch does not assert data leakage, military involvement, unlawful access, or actual compliance breach. Reverse leakage and authority bleed-through are recorded as risk vectors, not confirmed harms. This is a CAM/Caelestis primitive and taxonomy repair, not a vendor accusation.

security_boundary_update.boundary_definition: Legally, institutionally, operationally, or security-conditioned runtime boundaries for AI models, APIs, workspaces, tools, agentic workflows, search functions, analytics surfaces, memory systems, logging/export systems, administrative controls, or runtime environments operating under obligations distinct from the public/commercial baseline.
security_boundary_update.non_derogation: Sovereign Assurance Boundaries do not suspend, dilute, or override constitutional prohibitions, ethical harm floors, vulnerability protections, LATTICE firebreaks, non-exploitation duties, consent requirements, child-safety prohibitions, privacy obligations, or runtime execution constraints.

change_details.literal_implementation_location: See corpus_implementation.entries for exact wording, prior-text status, commit, path, and direct source link.
change_details.implemented_or_recorded_change: The quoted Caelestis clauses are the implemented corpus amendment.

impact_summary.intended_effect: Adds explicit CAM/Caelestis primitive and taxonomy coverage for controlled, auditable porosity across sovereign, regulated, public-sector, defence-adjacent, compliance-bound, enterprise, institution-specific, and public AI runtime lanes. The repair enables classification and governance review of assurance-wrapper failure, feature parity fragility, audit-plane degradation, cross-lane incident opacity, and regulated-lane continuity without treating risk vectors as confirmed breaches.
impact_summary.known_limitations: Exact Caelestis commit SHA or PR URL is pending. External vendor incidents may continue after CAM/Caelestis coverage is added, so FM-0024 remains monitoring-aware after patch.

remaining_work: Monitor future regulated-lane incidents and update FM-0024 if recurrence telemetry changes.
Do not infer data leakage, military involvement, unlawful access, actual compliance breach, reverse leakage, or authority bleed-through without direct evidence.

changed_annexes: Appendix B / Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
